---
title: "cleaninginr"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#loading all the packages

library(tidyverse)
library(rio)
library(devtools)
devtools::install_version("stringr", version = "1.4.1")
library(stringr)

#importing the cleaned words 
clean <- import("cleaned_evilwords.csv")
#only the ones with the spaces and then create the counted column
#and score pre n count 
clean <- clean %>% 
  select(spaces) %>% 
  mutate(counted = NA, score_pren = NA)

#import the tweets
label <- import("labeled_data.csv")


#start by dropping all that are class 2
#0 hate 1 offensive
label_off <- label %>% 
  filter(class < 2)

#hatespeech goes from 0 to 7 score 
#we normalize it to go from .125 for nothing to 1 for includes
label_off <- label_off %>% 
  mutate(scorehate = (hate_speech+1)/8)

#offensive langauge goes from 0 to 9
label_off <- label_off %>% 
  mutate(scoreoff = (offensive_language+1)/10)
#this weighs hate speach higher automatically! yay! 

#now getting count of all of our bad words <3 
for(i in 1:nrow(clean)) {
  #this detects and says true if it appears in the tweet!
  label_off$has_data <- str_detect(label_off$tweet, clean[i, 1])
  #now getting all of the rows that have true and make score
  label_off_true <- label_off %>% 
    filter(has_data == TRUE)
  clean[i, 3] <- (sum(label_off_true$scorehate)/nrow(label_off_true) + sum(label_off_true$scoreoff)/nrow(label_off_true))
  #now just getting the count of how many times it appears
  re <- label_off %>% 
    group_by(has_data) %>% 
    count()
  #putting it in clean! 
  clean[i, 2] <- re[2,2]
}

#the top possible highest score is 2
# highest 1.1250000 score pre n
# lowest 0.4250000 score pre n

#final score!!!! lets gooooo
clean_final <- clean %>% 
  mutate(normal = counted/11151)
clean_final <- clean_final %>% 
  mutate(final_score = score_pren + normal) %>% 
  select(spaces, final_score)

#export as a csv
write.csv(clean_final,"final_cleaned_scores.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
